demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged care facility, fully dependent for care

contact_nok.relationship: residential aged care nursing staff (informant on the call)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: He can't move, he can't swallow his pills, and he is shaking violently; he's completely locked up.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Nursing staff at the facility calling after hours: the resident has advanced Parkinson's and over the last 12 hours he has been choking on his tablets and has now missed several of his Parkinson's doses, and he has gone completely stiff and can't move.

symptom_narrative.onset: worsening over about 12 hours; now completely 'locked up'
symptom_narrative.character: started choking on his tablets and sips of water, then got stiffer and stiffer, shaking in the right hand, and now he cannot move at all in the bed
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: staff say he is the worst they have seen him — rigid, very distressed and frightened, unable to speak more than a whisper
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: whole body stiff; violent shaking of the right hand; saliva pooling in his mouth
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and worsening since he started missing the tablets
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — staff cannot get the tablets into him because he keeps choking
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: each missed dose seems to make the stiffness worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: drooling/pooling saliva, very soft whispery voice, mask-like face, high distress
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: staff say he gets stiff 'off' periods but never this severe or this stuck
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: acutely bedbound and completely dependent; cannot take oral tablets or thin fluids

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: advanced Parkinson's disease, diagnosed years ago
past_medical_history.how_managed: regular oral Parkinson's tablets (levodopa/carbidopa) several times a day

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: dizziness on standing (low blood pressure on standing)

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: several doses a day; has missed the last three because he keeps choking on them

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged care facility; nursing staff present and providing all care; family not on the call

communication_style.typical_message_style: nursing staff reporting on behalf of a frail resident who cannot self-report

objective_data_offered.value: oxygen level reads 96% on the facility's fingertip oximeter
temperature 36.9 on the facility thermometer — no fever
heart rate about 88 and blood pressure around 110 over 70 on the facility's monitor; breathing about 18 a minute
on video: violent tremor of the right hand, mask-like expressionless face, saliva pooling and drooling, lying rigid and unable to move in the bed

nursing staff report he has missed three Parkinson's doses over 12 hours because he keeps choking on the tablets and on thin fluids, is now completely 'locked up', very distressed, and can only whisper
a cup of water and several un-taken Parkinson's tablets sitting on the bedside table
